title: angelica maximowiczii
intro: Angelica maximowiczii, also known as Maximowicz's angelica or Korean angelica, is a perennial plant species native to East Asia, belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The plant's roots and rhizomes are traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, and are also said to have a positive impact on blood circulation and immune function. Herbal preparations of Angelica maximowiczii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various health issues.
